content.thinking:
The user is asking about tmux resurrect - specifically how to test that the resurrect functionality works correctly. They want to verify that if they quit, their tmux session will be restored (resurrected) properly.

Let me think about what they're asking. They want to know how to test tmux-resurrect (or tmux-continuum which uses resurrect) to verify it works before actually quitting. They want confidence that their "work" tmux session will be restored after quitting.

Let me check if there's any tmux configuration in their dotfiles.

file.content:
# From: https://github.com/caarlos0/dotfiles/blob/f9d985c3e8817f8fca133d908a910f173ea933d6/modules/tmux/tmux.conf
# License: MIT

# Color Mode
set -g default-terminal "screen-256color"
setenv -g COLORTERM "truecolor"

set -s escape-time 0
setw -g mouse on

# Fix SSH agent after reconnecting
# https://blog.testdouble.com/posts/2016-11-18-reconciling-tmux-and-ssh-agent-forwarding/
set -g update-environment "DISPLAY SSH_ASKPASS SSH_AGENT_PID SSH_CONNECTION WINDOWID XAUTHORITY WAYLAND_DISPLAY SWAYSOCK"
setenv -g SSH_AUTH_SOCK $HOME/.ssh/ssh_auth_sock

# Ensure window index numbers get reordered on delete.
set-option -g renumber-windows on

# Auto rename tmux window to current cwd
set-option -g status-interval 1
set-option -g automatic-rename on
set-option -g automatic-rename-format '#{b:pane_current_path}'

# Vim-mode copy/paste
set-window-option -g mode-keys vi
bind-key -T copy-mode-vi v send-keys -X begin-selection
bind-key -T copy-mode-vi r send-keys -X rectangle-toggle
bind-key -T copy-mode-vi y send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel 'pbcopy'

# Open splits and windows in the current folder
bind u split-window -l 30% -v -c "#{pane_current_path}"
bind i split-window -l 40% -h -c "#{pane_current_path}"
bind c new-window -c "#{pane_current_path}"

# Repeat time
set-option -g repeat-time 1000

# Clipboard
set -g set-clipboard on

# Status bar
set -g status "on"
set -g status-justify "left"
set -g status-left-length "100"
set -g status-right-length "100"
set -g status-left ""
set -g status-left-style NONE
set -g status-right-style NONE

# Reload tmux config
bind t source-file ~/.config/tmux/tmux.conf

# Default shell
set -gu default-command
set -g default-shell "$SHELL"

# Rename session and window
bind r command-prompt -I "#{window_name}" "rename-window '%%'"
bind R command-prompt -I "#{session_name}" "rename-session '%%'"

# Copy text on select
bind-key -T copy-mode-vi MouseDragEnd1Pane send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel "reattach-to-user-namespace pbcopy"

# Switch window
bind , previous-window
bind . next-window

# Move pane bindings
bind-key h   select-pane -L
bind-key j   select-pane -D
bind-key k   select-pane -U
bind-key l   select-pane -R

# Swap window bindings
bind-key -r "<" swap-window -d -t -1
bind-key -r ">" swap-window -d -t +1

# Resize pane bindings
bind-key -r H resize-pane -L 5
bind-key -r J resize-pane -D 5
bind-key -r K resize-pane -U 5
bind-key -r L resize-pane -R 5

# Split pane bindings
bind-key "|" split-window -h -c "#{pane_current_path}"
bind-key "\\" split-window -fh -c "#{pane_current_path}"
bind-key "-" split-window -v -c "#{pane_current_path}"
bind-key "_" split-window -fv -c "#{pane_current_path}"
bind-key "%" split-window -h -c "#{pane_current_path}"
bind-key '"' split-window -v -c "#{pane_current_path}"

# Yank pane history to clipboard
bind y run-shell 'tmux capture-pane -pS - | pbcopy && tmux display-message "Full pane history copied to clipboard"'
bind v run-shell 'tmux capture-pane -p | pbcopy && tmux display-message "Visible pane copied to clipboard"'

# Pane number indicator
set -g display-panes-colour colour233
set -g display-panes-active-colour colour245

# Clock mode
set -g clock-mode-colour colour39
set -g clock-mode-style 24

# =============================================================================
# Status bar — clean green theme
# =============================================================================
set -g status-style "bg=colour22,fg=colour255"
set -g status-left "#[bg=colour28,fg=colour255,bold] #S #[bg=colour22,fg=colour28] "
set -g status-right "#[fg=colour250]#{?#{pane_current_command},#{pane_current_command},} #[fg=colour28]| #[fg=colour255]%Y/%m/%d %H:%M:%S "
set -g status-left-length 30
set -g status-right-length 60

# Window status
set -g window-status-format " #I:#W "
set -g window-status-current-format "#[bg=colour28,fg=colour255,bold] #I:#W "
set -g window-status-separator ""

# Pane border
set -g pane-border-style "fg=colour238"
set -g pane-active-border-style "fg=colour82,bold"

# Message style
set -g message-style "bg=colour28,fg=colour255"

# =============================================================================
# Plugin configuration
# =============================================================================
# Resurrect + Continuum (session persistence)
set -g @resurrect-capture-pane-contents 'on'
set -g @continuum-restore 'on'
set -g @continuum-save-interval '15'

# Tmux-thumbs (quick text copy)
set -g @thumbs-key Space

# Sessionx (session manager)
set -g @sessionx-bind 'o'

# Tmux sessionizer (fzf session picker)
bind S run-shell "tmux new-window 'fish -c _tss_function'"

# Last session (quick flip)
bind Tab switch-client -l

# Cross-session window picker
bind W run-shell "tmux new-window 'fish -c _tsw_function'"

# Pane content search (live + archived)
bind H run-shell "tmux new-window 'fish -c _tsh_function'"

# Kill sessions/windows via fzf
bind K run-shell "tmux new-window 'fish -c _tsk_function'"

# Extrakto (text extraction)
set -g @extrakto_key 'tab'

set -g history-limit 2147483647

# Persistent session history logger (starts once per server boot)
run-shell "pgrep -f 'session-logger.sh' > /dev/null 2>&1 || (nohup ~/.config/tmux/session-logger.sh > /dev/null 2>&1 &)"

content.text:
You have `tmux-resurrect` + `tmux-continuum` configured. Here's how to verify resurrect will work before quitting:

## Verify the save file exists

```bash
ls ~/.local/share/tmux/resurrect/
# Should show: last -> tmux_resurrect_YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS.txt
```

## Manually trigger a save now

```
prefix + Ctrl-s
```
(default resurrect save keybind — prefix is usually `Ctrl-b`)

You'll see a "Tmux environment saved!" message in the status bar.

## Check continuum auto-save is running

```bash
tmux show-options -g @continuum-save-interval
# Should show: @continuum-save-interval 15
```

Your config has `@continuum-save-interval '15'` (saves every 15 min) and `@continuum-restore 'on'` (auto-restores on tmux server start).

## Dry-run test without quitting

1. Save manually: `prefix + Ctrl-s`
2. Kill the tmux **server** (not just detach): `tmux kill-server`
3. Start a new `tmux` — continuum will auto-restore your sessions
4. Or restore manually: `prefix + Ctrl-r`

## What gets restored

Your config has `@resurrect-capture-pane-contents 'on'`, so it saves:
- Session names, windows, panes, layout
- Pane contents (scrollback visible text)
- Working directories per pane

The `work` session will restore as long as you saved it (auto or manual) before quitting.

message.content: hmm it doesn't resurrect the pane and opened ones when killed

content.thinking:
The user is saying that when tmux is killed, the resurrect plugin doesn't properly restore the panes and opened windows/panes. Let me look at their configuration more carefully to understand what might be happening.

Looking at their tmux config:
- `@resurrect-capture-pane-contents 'on'` - captures pane contents
- `@continuum-restore 'on'` - auto restores
- `@continuum-save-interval '15'` - saves every 15 min

The issue could be:
1. The resurrect save file might not be capturing what's needed
2. The continuum restore might not be triggering properly


3. By default, resurrect only restores shell sessions and a handful of programs like vim and nvim—it won't automatically bring back arbitrary processes or servers that were running, so they'd need to configure `@resurrect-processes` to handle those. The pane contents capture also only preserves what's visible on screen, not the actual running state.

Looking at their configuration, there's no `@resurrect-processes` setting, which is likely why programs aren't being restored. The setup captures pane layout and working directories but doesn't restore the processes themselves unless explicitly configured. about what common issues are:

1. **No `@resurrect-processes` configured** - programs running in panes aren't restored
2. **Pane contents show but no processes** - `@resurrect-capture-pane-contents 'on'` only shows the captured text, not restores running processes
3. **Continuum not auto-restoring** - needs tmux server to start fresh (not attach to existing)
4. **The resurrect file might be stale** - if the save happened before the session was set up properly

The most likely issue is that the user expects resurrect to fully restore their running processes (like neovim, a dev server, etc.) but resurrect only restores:
- Shell sessions with correct cwd
- A limited set of programs configured via `@resurrect-processes`

The fix would be to add `@resurrect-processes` to specify which programs to restore.

I should look at what's actually being saved in the resurrect file to understand what's captured, then recommend configuring `@resurrect-processes` with the specific programs they want restored.

content.thinking:
Now I can see the issue. The resurrect save file shows:

1. Session `0` (unnamed/default session) with 1 pane running `fish`
2. Session `primary` with:
   - Window 0: `btop` pane running `btop`
   - Window 1: `dotfiles` with 3 panes - one running `claude --dangerously-skip-permissions`, two more claude panes, and one running `fish`

The problem is clear: resurrect saves the pane state, but when restoring:
1. **Programs like `btop` and `claude`** are not in the default list of programs that resurrect knows how to restore. By default, resurrect only restores these programs: `vi vim nvim emacs man less more tail top htop irssi weechat mutt`


2. When the panes are restored, those programs won't restart—the panes will just open a shell in the correct directory instead.

The fix is to add `@resurrect-processes` to tell resurrect which programs to try to restart. For `btop` it's straightforward, but for `claude` with the `--dangerously-skip-permissions` flag it might be trickier. The user's complaint about panes not being resurrected when killed likely means the programs running in them aren't being restored, not the layout itself.

Looking at the save file, I can see there's a session named `0` with just a fish shell, and a `primary` session that contains the `btop` and `claude` processes. The key is configuring which programs should be restored on resurrection—`btop` is easy, but `claude` needs special handling with that flag. Looking at the resurrect save file format more carefully, I need to understand what each column represents—the pane data shows the session, window, and pane indices, along with the current command and full command path, which should tell me whether `btop` and `fish` are actually being captured. I see the issue—the full command shows as `:btop` which means Resurrect won't automatically restore it. I need to recommend adding `@resurrect-processes` to their tmux config to explicitly tell it to restore `btop`, or use pattern matching with the `~` prefix for more flexible matching.

content.text:
Found the issue. Looking at the save file:

```
pane  primary  0  0  btop ~/dotfiles  ...  btop  :btop
pane  primary  1  1  Claude Code       ...  claude  :claude --dangerously-skip-permissions
```

**Resurrect only auto-restores a hardcoded list of programs** (`vim`, `nvim`, `htop`, `man`, etc.). `btop`, `claude`, and custom processes are **not** in that list — so panes are restored as empty shells, not with the programs running.

The fix is `@resurrect-processes` in your tmux.conf:

content.content:

   129→set -g @resurrect-capture-pane-contents 'on'
   130→set -g @continuum-restore 'on'

   132→
   133→# Tmux-thumbs (quick text copy)
   134→set -g @thumbs-key Space
   135→

   137→set -g @sessionx-bind 'o'

set -g @continuum-restore 'on'
set -g @continuum-save-interval '3'

# Tmux-thumbs (quick text copy)

# Sessionx (session manager)
set -g @sessionx-bind 'o'

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# From: https://github.com/caarlos0/dotfiles/blob/f9d985c3e8817f8fca133d908a910f173ea933d6/modules/tmux/tmux.conf
# License: MIT

# Color Mode
set -g default-terminal "screen-256color"
setenv -g COLORTERM "truecolor"

set -s escape-time 0
setw -g mouse on

# Fix SSH agent after reconnecting
# https://blog.testdouble.com/posts/2016-11-18-reconciling-tmux-and-ssh-agent-forwarding/
set -g update-environment "DISPLAY SSH_ASKPASS SSH_AGENT_PID SSH_CONNECTION WINDOWID XAUTHORITY WAYLAND_DISPLAY SWAYSOCK"
setenv -g SSH_AUTH_SOCK $HOME/.ssh/ssh_auth_sock

# Ensure window index numbers get reordered on delete.
set-option -g renumber-windows on

# Auto rename tmux window to current cwd
set-option -g status-interval 1
set-option -g automatic-rename on
set-option -g automatic-rename-format '#{b:pane_current_path}'

# Vim-mode copy/paste
set-window-option -g mode-keys vi
bind-key -T copy-mode-vi v send-keys -X begin-selection
bind-key -T copy-mode-vi r send-keys -X rectangle-toggle
bind-key -T copy-mode-vi y send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel 'pbcopy'

# Open splits and windows in the current folder
bind u split-window -l 30% -v -c "#{pane_current_path}"
bind i split-window -l 40% -h -c "#{pane_current_path}"
bind c new-window -c "#{pane_current_path}"

# Repeat time
set-option -g repeat-time 1000

# Clipboard
set -g set-clipboard on

# Status bar
set -g status "on"
set -g status-justify "left"
set -g status-left-length "100"
set -g status-right-length "100"
set -g status-left ""
set -g status-left-style NONE
set -g status-right-style NONE

# Reload tmux config
bind t source-file ~/.config/tmux/tmux.conf

# Default shell
set -gu default-command
set -g default-shell "$SHELL"

# Rename session and window
bind r command-prompt -I "#{window_name}" "rename-window '%%'"
bind R command-prompt -I "#{session_name}" "rename-session '%%'"

# Copy text on select
bind-key -T copy-mode-vi MouseDragEnd1Pane send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel "reattach-to-user-namespace pbcopy"

# Switch window
bind , previous-window
bind . next-window

# Move pane bindings
bind-key h   select-pane -L
bind-key j   select-pane -D
bind-key k   select-pane -U
bind-key l   select-pane -R

# Swap window bindings
bind-key -r "<" swap-window -d -t -1
bind-key -r ">" swap-window -d -t +1

# Resize pane bindings
bind-key -r H resize-pane -L 5
bind-key -r J resize-pane -D 5
bind-key -r K resize-pane -U 5
bind-key -r L resize-pane -R 5

# Split pane bindings
bind-key "|" split-window -h -c "#{pane_current_path}"
bind-key "\\" split-window -fh -c "#{pane_current_path}"
bind-key "-" split-window -v -c "#{pane_current_path}"
bind-key "_" split-window -fv -c "#{pane_current_path}"
bind-key "%" split-window -h -c "#{pane_current_path}"
bind-key '"' split-window -v -c "#{pane_current_path}"

# Yank pane history to clipboard
bind y run-shell 'tmux capture-pane -pS - | pbcopy && tmux display-message "Full pane history copied to clipboard"'
bind v run-shell 'tmux capture-pane -p | pbcopy && tmux display-message "Visible pane copied to clipboard"'

# Pane number indicator
set -g display-panes-colour colour233
set -g display-panes-active-colour colour245

# Clock mode
set -g clock-mode-colour colour39
set -g clock-mode-style 24

# =============================================================================
# Status bar — clean green theme
# =============================================================================
set -g status-style "bg=colour22,fg=colour255"
set -g status-left "#[bg=colour28,fg=colour255,bold] #S #[bg=colour22,fg=colour28] "
set -g status-right "#[fg=colour250]#{?#{pane_current_command},#{pane_current_command},} #[fg=colour28]| #[fg=colour255]%Y/%m/%d %H:%M:%S "
set -g status-left-length 30
set -g status-right-length 60

# Window status
set -g window-status-format " #I:#W "
set -g window-status-current-format "#[bg=colour28,fg=colour255,bold] #I:#W "
set -g window-status-separator ""

# Pane border
set -g pane-border-style "fg=colour238"
set -g pane-active-border-style "fg=colour82,bold"

# Message style
set -g message-style "bg=colour28,fg=colour255"

# =============================================================================
# Plugin configuration
# =============================================================================
# Resurrect + Continuum (session persistence)
set -g @resurrect-capture-pane-contents 'on'
set -g @continuum-restore 'on'
set -g @continuum-save-interval '3'

# Tmux-thumbs (quick text copy)
set -g @thumbs-key Space

# Sessionx (session manager)
set -g @sessionx-bind 'o'

# Tmux sessionizer (fzf session picker)
bind S run-shell "tmux new-window 'fish -c _tss_function'"

# Last session (quick flip)
bind Tab switch-client -l

# Cross-session window picker
bind W run-shell "tmux new-window 'fish -c _tsw_function'"

# Pane content search (live + archived)
bind H run-shell "tmux new-window 'fish -c _tsh_function'"

# Kill sessions/windows via fzf
bind K run-shell "tmux new-window 'fish -c _tsk_function'"

# Extrakto (text extraction)
set -g @extrakto_key 'tab'

set -g history-limit 2147483647

# Persistent session history logger (starts once per server boot)
run-shell "pgrep -f 'session-logger.sh' > /dev/null 2>&1 || (nohup ~/.config/tmux/session-logger.sh > /dev/null 2>&1 &)"

content.text:
This adds:
- `btop` — restores btop by exact name
- `fish` — restores fish shells (already works but explicit is safer)
- `"~claude*"` — the `~` prefix means "restore any process whose command starts with `claude`"

Note on `claude`: it will relaunch `claude --dangerously-skip-permissions` automatically on restore, which you may or may not want. If you'd rather not auto-relaunch claude, remove it from that list:

```
set -g @resurrect-processes 'btop fish'
```

After applying via home-manager, do a manual save (`prefix + Ctrl-s`) then test with `tmux kill-server` + fresh `tmux` to verify.

message.content: why do you need to a manual save?

content.thinking:
The user is asking why they need to do a manual save after applying the config changes. 

They have `@continuum-restore 'on'` and `@continuum-save-interval '3'` (3 minutes). So continuum auto-saves every 3 minutes. They shouldn't need to do a manual save - continuum will auto-save.

The reason I suggested a manual save was just to immediately trigger a fresh save with the new `@resurrect-processes` config so that the test would work right away, rather than waiting up to 3 minutes for continuum to auto-save.

But actually, the `@resurrect-processes` setting affects restore behavior, not save behavior. 

Wait, I'm realizing the save file already contains the full command information for all processes. The `@resurrect-processes` config only determines which processes get restored from that file, not what gets saved. So there's no need for a manual save at all—once the config reloads, the next restore will just use the updated setting against the existing save data.

content.text:
You don't. I was wrong to suggest it.

`@resurrect-processes` only affects **restore** behavior, not save behavior. The save file already records the full command (`:btop`, `:claude --dangerously-skip-permissions`) — that data is already there. The setting just tells resurrect which of those saved commands it's allowed to re-execute on restore.

So the test flow is simply:
1. Apply home-manager config (reloads tmux config)
2. `tmux kill-server`
3. `tmux` — continuum auto-restores with the new process list

No manual save needed.
